    {"target": "30", "sources": ["13", "14", "15", "16", "17", "18", "19", "20+"], "op": "overlap_weighted", "source_spans": [[13, 13], [14, 14], [15, 15], [16, 16], [17, 17], [18, 18], [19, 19], [20, null]]},
    {"target": "40", "sources": ["20+"], "op": "overlap_weighted", "source_spans": [[20, null]]}
  ],
  "derive": null,
  "transpose": true,
  "notes": "The document publishes termination rates in Table B-2 (page 56) as a service-only list (rate by completed years of service), separately for General and Safety groups; there is no age dimension, so rates are age-invariant in the source and every target age row maps to the same blended service-based rate. Table B-2 is transcribed as printed (service rows x two group columns) and transpose=true so code maps the printed service rows onto target service columns. The two group columns (General/Safety) are blended by population using group_weighted, with weights from the General (Table A-9, index 1) and Safety (Table A-11, index 2) active-member distribution headcount tables. Those weight tables are Age x Years-of-Service grids; code looks up each group's headcount at the output cell's coordinates. Values in Table B-2 are printed as percentages (values_unit not set as percent because rates are printed with '%' symbol - transcribed as the numeric value shown, e.g. 10.00 meaning 10.00%). NOTE: I set values_unit to percent on the rate table via the numeric transcription; add values_unit='percent' handling - the numbers are percent. Service target bins beyond printed: source lists service up to '20+' which is open-ended, so target col '30' (13-30) blends single-year rates 13-19 plus the 20+ rate, and target col '40' (31-40) copies the 20+ open-ended rate. The rate table row for service 0 is intentionally not used since target service year 0 is not part of the template; target col '1' uses service-year-1 rate. Weight-table col_labels use printed service bins; group_weighted looks up by output cell service coordinate."
}
